purpose: Randomly re-audit pull-request control claims after review or merge and detect missed, regressed, stale, or falsely satisfied invariants.

selection.population: open and recently merged pull requests carrying a pr-ontology declaration
selection.seed: UTC audit date plus repository identity

audit_rules: Re-derive required controls from the declared PR category.
Reject missing, duplicate, unknown, or unsupported ontology fields.
Re-check every sampled PASS control against its cited evidence.
Verify referenced repository paths exist at the audited PR head.
Verify cited commit SHAs are reachable from the audited PR head when reachability is claimed.
Verify exact-head certification claims have successful workflow evidence attached to the audited head.
Verify lifecycle claims do not exceed evidence: candidate, accepted, released, and deployed remain distinct.
Preserve the sampled PR numbers, head SHAs, seed, checks, findings, and API evidence in a retained report.

escalation.one_or_two_findings: Open or update a governance investigation issue and block acceptance, release, or deployment claims for affected pull requests until technically resolved.
escalation.three_or_more_findings: Open or update a control-system rework issue, classify the current ontology/control package as unreliable, and require Architect-led root-cause analysis and redesign before further promotions.
escalation.zero_findings: Retain the report as audit evidence without changing product priority or lifecycle state.

prohibitions: The Inspector may not resolve review threads merely because a finding is categorized or outdated.
The Inspector may not modify implementation code, approve releases, or weaken required controls.
Random selection may not be used to exclude known failures from remediation.
